entry.title: Re: Requirement to Display Two Sales Org Addresses on MSDS
entry.content:
Hello @christoph_bergemann ,

Our custom driven table has Country code and Sales Org as Primary field so we cannot add multiple Sales Org to a single country code. I believe when I solution was first finalized in the past we had agreed with one Sales Org per Country. Now after few years we have a change request to display Multiple address for one Country. Changing the table structure now means we will have to relook at its implications in all other areas of the programming.

Regards,
Rohan

entry.title: Re: is there any way to call table in public cloud?
entry.content:
Hi nirojan_vel,

You won't have access to any tables directly programmatically or through business logic - you could check the Customer Data Browser to find some exposed tables.

That being said, you will need to find a CDS View

The View Browser app will let you search on a field, you can find PERNR for example, but none of the views are released for Key User or Developer Extensibility.

Because those are potentially personal data, SAP takes steps to protect the data due to privacy laws.

You can check the help, for example the HR related views:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/8aeb6c48ea1b451aa27e34f1d025191e.html?locale=en-US

The Person Work Agreement has Personnel Number, if that is one you are looking for.

Workforce Person might also be useful for you as it has UserId.

Please let us know if this helps and what you find

Thank you
Jerry

entry.title: Dynamic Configuration in SAP Integration Suite: One Tenant, Multiple Environments
entry.content:
Introduction

In today's business landscape, efficiency and cost optimization are paramount. For many organizations leveraging SAP technologies, this often means consolidating resources where possible. SAP Integration Suite is a powerful platform, but licensing and infrastructure costs can lead customers to explore strategies like utilizing a single tenant to handle integrations connected to different backend SAP landscapes – typically Development (DEV), Test (TEST), and even Production (PROD) for specific, lower-impact scenarios.

This approach presents a challenge: How do you configure a single integration flow artifact, deployed on this single tenant, to dynamically connect to the correct SAP system (DEV, TEST, or PROD) for each specific execution? We want to avoid maintaining multiple copies of the iflow logic or resorting to cumbersome manual configuration changes.

In this blog post, we will explore a practical and robust pattern to implement dynamic configuration in your SAP Integration Suite iflows. The core idea is to have one central configuration mechanism (a Groovy script with a configuration map in our example) driven by one simple property (ENV), allowing a single iflow artifact to serve multiple backend environments when deployed as different instances configured for each environment.

The Problem: One Iflow Artifact, Multiple Backend Destinations

By default, when you configure a receiver adapter in SAP Integration Suite (like an HTTP or OData adapter), you typically specify a static endpoint URL and select a specific Credential Name (alias) from your tenant's Security Material.

 

This works perfectly if your iflow always connects to the same target system. However, consider the scenario where you want to deploy the same integration flow artifact onto a single tenant, but need one deployed instance of it to talk to your SAP S/4HANA DEV system, another instance to talk to the TEST system, and maybe even a third instance to talk to PROD.

The goal is to maintain just one source iflow artifact. We want to avoid:

  • Creating and maintaining separate iflow artifacts for each environment (e.g., MyIflow_DEV, MyIflow_TEST, MyIflow_PROD), leading to duplicated logic.
  • Manually changing the adapter configuration on the deployed iflow instance whenever you want to point it to a different environment.

We need a way for the single iflow artifact to behave differently at runtime based on which environment it is intended for in that specific execution.

The Solution: Dynamic Configuration Driven by an Environment Property

The solution centers around using a message property as an environment identifier to dynamically look up connection details from a central configuration source within the iflow itself.

The pattern involves these key steps:

  1. Identify the target environment for the current execution. We will store this as a message property, for example, ENV.
  2. Based on the value of the ENV property (e.g., "DEV", "TEST", or "PROD"), look up the necessary environment-specific configuration details (like the SAP system URL and the required Credential Alias) from a defined source.
  3. Use the looked-up URL and Credential Alias to dynamically configure the receiver adapter just before the outbound call.

This way, the core integration logic resides in a single, maintainable iflow artifact. The differentiation between environments is handled by setting the ENV property for each specific deployed instance and using a lookup mechanism.

Implementation Steps

Let's walk through how to implement this pattern using a simple example iflow.

1. The Integration Flow Structure

Here is the basic iflow diagram illustrating the components involved:

 

  • Start Timer: Triggers the integration flow execution (can be any other sender adapter).
  • Init Variables: Reads the environment identifier configured for this specific deployed instance and sets it as a message property (ENV).
  • Global Config: Contains the core logic to map the ENV property value (e.g., "DEV") to the specific SAP system URL and Credential Alias. This acts as our central configuration store within the iflow design.
  • Call SAP: This is the receiver adapter (HTTP, OData, etc.) which is configured to use the dynamic properties set in the previous step.
  • End: The integration flow completes.

2. Setting the Environment Identifier (ENV Property) per Deployment Instance

This step is crucial and explains how a single iflow artifact is configured differently for DEV, TEST, or PROD runs on the same tenant. We use Externalized Parameters for this.

  • In your single iflow artifact's design, go to the "Externalized Parameters" tab. Define a parameter that will hold the environment identifier value:
    • Name: ENV
    • Type: java.lang.String
    • Default Value: (Optional, but setting a default like "DEV" can be helpful during initial testing)
  • Add a Content Modifier step right after your Start event (or wherever you initialize properties). Name it "Init Variables".
  • In the "Message Properties" tab of the Content Modifier, configure a new property that reads the value of the EnvIdentifier externalized parameter:
    • Name: ENV (This specific name is what our Groovy script will look for)
    • Type: java.lang.String
    • Value: By “{{“ “}}” define a parameter that will hold the environment identifier value, for example ENV.

 

 

Now, here's how you differentiate the behavior without changing the iflow artifact itself:

  • You deploy your single iflow artifact to your SAP Integration Suite tenant.
  • During the deployment process, or by selecting the deployed iflow instance and going to its "Configure" tab, you provide the specific value for the EnvIdentifier parameter.
    • To target the DEV system, you deploy an instance and set ENV to DEV.
    • To target the TEST system, you deploy another instance of the same artifact and set ENV to TEST.
    • To target the PROD system, you deploy a third instance of the same artifact and set ENV to PROD.

 

 When each specific deployed instance of the iflow runs, the "Init Variables" step will set the ENV message property to the value (DEV, TEST, or PROD) that was configured for that particular instance during deployment.

3. The Global Config (Script collection)

The "Global Config" step contains the central lookup logic. It reads the ENV property and finds the corresponding connection details.

Create a new package called “Global Config”. 

In the package create a new Script collection artifact.

Inside the newly created collection create a new groovy script and paste the following code into the script editor:

/*
 * Groovy Script to determine environment-specific SAP configuration
 * based on a message property 'ENV'.
 *
 * It retrieves 'ENV', looks up URL and Credential Alias from a map,
 * and sets them as message properties GC_SAP_URL and GC_SAP_CREDEDITAL.
 *
 * Includes robust error handling for missing or invalid ENV property
 * and missing configuration entries.
 */

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;

def Message processData(Message message) {

    def properties = message.getProperties();

    // 1. Retrieve the ENV property - Assumed to be set earlier from an Externalized Parameter
    def ENV_raw = properties.get("ENV");

    if (ENV_raw == null || ENV_raw.toString().trim().isEmpty()) {
        def errorMessage = "Message property 'ENV' is missing or empty. Cannot determine environment configuration. Ensure the 'EnvIdentifier' externalized parameter is set for this deployed instance.";
        println("Error: " + errorMessage); // Log error to trace
        throw new Exception(errorMessage); // Stop processing with a clear error message
    }

    // Trim and convert to uppercase for consistent lookup
    def ENV = ENV_raw.toString().trim().toUpperCase();
    println("Info: Retrieved ENV property: " + ENV); // Log the found ENV

    // 2. Define the central configuration map - Contains details for all environments
    // IMPORTANT: Hardcoded here, consider Value Mapping for larger configs
    def config = [
        DEV: [
            SAP_URL: "https://dev.sap.com/your/service/path", // Replace with actual DEV URL
            SAP_CREDEDITAL: "DEV_USER_CREDENTIAL" // Replace with actual DEV Credential Alias
        ],
        TEST: [
            SAP_URL: "https://test.sap.com/your/service/path", // Replace with actual TEST URL
            SAP_CREDEDITAL: "TEST_USER_CREDENTIAL" // Replace with actual TEST Credential Alias
        ],
        PROD: [
            SAP_URL: "https://prod.sap.com/your/service/path", // Replace with actual PROD URL
            SAP_CREDEDITAL: "PROD_USER_CREDENTIAL" // Replace with actual PROD Credential Alias
        ]
        // Add more environments as needed, ensuring corresponding Credential Aliases exist in Security Material
        // e.g., QA: [ SAP_URL: "https://qa.sap.com/your/service/path", SAP_CREDEDITAL: "QA_USER_CREDENTIAL" ]
    ];

    // 3. Lookup configuration based on ENV and add error handling
    def envConfig = config.get(ENV); // Using get() returns null if key doesn't exist

    if (envConfig == null) {
        def errorMessage = "Environment '" + ENV + "' not found in the script's configuration map. Please check the 'ENV' property value or the script's configuration. Valid environments are: " + config.keySet().join(", ");
        println("Error: " + errorMessage);
        throw new Exception(errorMessage);
    }
    println("Info: Found configuration block for environment: " + ENV);

    // 4. Retrieve specific configuration values and add error handling for missing/empty keys
    def sapUrl = envConfig.get("SAP_URL");
    def sapCredential = envConfig.get("SAP_CREDEDITAL");

    if (sapUrl == null || sapUrl.toString().trim().isEmpty()) {
        def errorMessage = "Configuration key 'SAP_URL' is missing or empty for environment '" + ENV + "'.";
        println("Error: " + errorMessage);
        throw new Exception(errorMessage);
    }
     println("Info: Found SAP_URL: " + sapUrl);

    if (sapCredential == null || sapCredential.toString().trim().isEmpty()) {
        def errorMessage = "Configuration key 'SAP_CREDEDITAL' is missing or empty for environment '" + ENV + "'.";
        println("Error: " + errorMessage);
        throw new Exception(errorMessage);
    }
    println("Info: Found SAP_CREDEDITAL: " + sapCredential);


    // 5. Set message properties used by the outbound adapter
    // Use distinct property names (e.g., GC_ prefix for Global Config)
    message.setProperty("GC_SAP_URL", sapUrl);
    message.setProperty("GC_SAP_CREDEDITAL", sapCredential);

    println("Info: Successfully set properties GC_SAP_URL and GC_SAP_CREDEDITAL for environment " + ENV + ".");

    return message;
}

Explanation of the Groovy Script:

  • Reads the ENV property value that was set based on the deployed instance's configuration.
  • Contains a central config map that holds the connection details (URL and Credential Alias) for all environments (DEV, TEST, PROD, etc.). This map is the single source of truth for environment-specific connection details within this iflow artifact.
  • Looks up the configuration details specifically for the environment indicated by the ENV property.
  • Sets these retrieved URL and Credential Alias values as new message properties (GC_SAP_URL, GC_SAP_CREDEDITAL).
  • Includes robust error handling.

Important: Ensure that the Credential Aliases defined in the script's config map exist and are correctly configured in your SAP Integration Suite tenant's "Security Material -> Security Artifacts".

4. Call script collection script

Now we add reference to created Script Collection with Global Config script.

 

Add a Groovy Script step after "Init Variables" and choose script from our Script Collection.

 

5. Configuring the Receiver Adapter (Call SAP)

Configure your outbound adapter ("Call SAP") to use the message properties set by the Groovy script dynamically at runtime.

For an HTTP Receiver adapter:

  • In the Address field, enter ${property.GC_SAP_URL}.
  • In the Authentication section:
    • Choose Credential Name.
    • In the Credential Name field, enter ${property.GC_SAP_CREDEDITAL}.

 

This configuration tells the adapter to fetch the necessary connection details from the message properties just before making the outbound call, ensuring it connects to the correct backend system for that specific iflow execution.

 

Benefits of this Approach

  • Cost Efficiency: Successfully run integrations for multiple backend landscapes on a single SAP Integration Suite tenant by deploying multiple instances of the same iflow artifact.
  • Simplified Development: Develop and maintain only one core iflow artifact and one central configuration map within that artifact (or linked Value Mapping) for your integration logic, regardless of how many environments it connects to.
  • Easier Maintenance: Updates to the core logic are made in one place. Configuration changes (like updating a URL or adding a new environment) require modifying only the central configuration map (in the script or Value Mapping) and redeploying the single iflow artifact.
  • Controlled Deployment: Differentiate deployed instances solely by setting an externalized parameter during deployment or configuration, allowing for clear identification (e.g., naming deployed instances "MyIflow - DEV", "MyIflow - TEST").
  • Reduced Error Potential: Avoid manual, error-prone configuration changes on deployed artifacts by driving configuration from a controlled parameter.

Considerations and Alternatives

  • Configuration Location: While the config map is hardcoded in the Groovy script for simplicity, for larger or more frequently changing configurations, storing this map in a Value Mapping artifact is highly recommended. The Groovy script would then read from the Value Mapping. This externalizes the configuration lookup data from the script code itself, allowing updates without script modification.
  • Complexity: This pattern adds a layer of indirection compared to static configuration. Evaluate if the benefits outweigh the complexity for your specific use case.
  • Extensibility: This pattern is easily extended to dynamically configure other environment-specific parameters (e.g., directory paths, filenames, specific header values) by adding them to the config map and retrieving them in the script.

Conclusion

Handling connections to multiple backend environments (DEV, TEST, PROD) from a single SAP Integration Suite tenant is a common requirement driven by cost and efficiency goals. By maintaining one core integration flow artifact, implementing a central configuration lookup driven by a simple environment property (ENV), and leveraging externalized parameters to set this property on different deployed instances, you can create flexible, maintainable, and cost-effective integration solutions.

This pattern allows your single iflow artifact to dynamically adapt its outbound connections at runtime, proving that one tenant can indeed effectively serve multiple landscapes with a smart configuration strategy.

Give this pattern a try in your next multi-environment integration scenario!

entry.title: Re: No payment medium created due to invalid bank or bran...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

If you followed SAP Note 3478710 and maintained the branch data using the "Manage Banks - Master Data" app, but you're still seeing the error, here are a few things to double-check:

Key things to review:

1. Complete Branch Info

    • In “Manage Banks,” make sure the branch number, BIC/SWIFT, and any country-specific fields (like Fedwire ID in the US) are fully filled in.

2. Business Partner Bank Details

    • Go to “Maintain Business Partner,” check the bank data under “Payment Transactions.”
    • The bank key should match the one you maintained, with correct country and account number.

3. Bank Account Assignment

    • In “Manage Bank Accounts,” ensure the correct bank branch is assigned to your house bank account, and linked to the right company code.

4. Context of the Error

    • Is it happening during a payment run, file generation, or something else? That can hint at missing fields like IBAN or incorrect payment method config.

5. Check for Note Updates

    • Make sure you’re looking at the latest version of Note 3478710. SAP may have added new steps or corrections.

I hope this will help find a solution to your problem.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: BUDGET QUERY
entry.content:
Good afternoon, esteemed experts. I am looking to create a query for the Budget section that compares the actual budget and the amount when a purchase order is generated. The idea is to validate whether it falls within a certain range — for example, from 1 to 200, or from 200 to 400 as an excess over the budget. The issue is that the query returns too many results. Do you have any ideas on how to structure it?

  SELECT

  *

  FROM(

  SELECT

  T1.AcctCode,

  T1.OcrCode,

  SUM(T1.LineTotal) Total

  FROM ODRF T0

  INNER JOIN DRF1 T1 ON T1.DocEntry = T0.DocEntry

  WHERE T0.DocEntry = @List_of_cols_val_tab_del AND T0.ObjType = '22' AND T0.DocType = 'S'

  GROUP BY

  T1.AcctCode,

  T1.OcrCode

  )A

  INNER JOIN (

  SELECT

  T0.AbsId,

  T0.OcrCode,

  T1.AcctCode,

  T2.DebLTotal

  FROM OBGS T0

  INNER JOIN OBGT T1 ON T1.Instance = T0.AbsId

  INNER JOIN BGT1 T2 ON T2.BudgId = T1.AbsId AND T2.Line_ID = (MONTH(@FechaPRESUPUESTO)-1)

  WHERE YEAR(T0.FinancYear) = YEAR(@FechaPRESUPUESTO)

  )B ON B.AcctCode = A.AcctCode AND B.OcrCode = A.OcrCode

  INNER JOIN(

  SELECT

  Account,

  ProfitCode,

  SUM(Debit-Credit) Used

  FROM JDT1 T0

  WHERE (YEAR(T0.RefDate) = YEAR(@FechaPRESUPUESTO)) AND (MONTH(T0.RefDate) = MONTH(@FechaPRESUPUESTO))

  GROUP BY

  Account,

  ProfitCode

  )C ON C.Account = A.AcctCode AND C.ProfitCode = A.OcrCode

  WHERE (A.Total + ISNULL(C.Used,'0')) > B.DebLTotal

)

BEGIN

  SET @CuentaOC = (

  SELECT

  TOP 1 CAST(D.AcctName AS VARCHAR(20)) + ' - ' + CAST(A.OcrCode AS VARCHAR(10))

  FROM(

  SELECT

  T1.AcctCode,

  T1.OcrCode,

  SUM(T1.LineTotal) Total

  FROM ODRF T0

  INNER JOIN DRF1 T1 ON T1.DocEntry = T0.DocEntry

  WHERE T0.DocEntry = @List_of_cols_val_tab_del AND T0.ObjType = '22' AND T0.DocType = 'S'

  GROUP BY

  T1.AcctCode,

  T1.OcrCode

  )A

  INNER JOIN (

  SELECT

  T0.AbsId,

  T0.OcrCode,

  T1.AcctCode,

  T2.DebLTotal

  FROM OBGS T0

  INNER JOIN OBGT T1 ON T1.Instance = T0.AbsId

  INNER JOIN BGT1 T2 ON T2.BudgId = T1.AbsId AND T2.Line_ID = (MONTH(@FechaPRESUPUESTO)-1)

  WHERE YEAR(T0.FinancYear) = YEAR(@FechaPRESUPUESTO)

  )B ON B.AcctCode = A.AcctCode AND B.OcrCode = A.OcrCode

  INNER JOIN(

  SELECT

  Account,

  ProfitCode,

  SUM(Debit-Credit) Used

  FROM JDT1 T0

  WHERE (YEAR(T0.RefDate) = YEAR(@FechaPRESUPUESTO)) AND (MONTH(T0.RefDate) = MONTH(@FechaPRESUPUESTO))

  GROUP BY

  Account,

  ProfitCode

  )C ON C.Account = A.AcctCode AND C.ProfitCode = A.OcrCode

  INNER JOIN OACT D ON D.AcctCode = A.AcctCode

  WHERE (A.Total + ISNULL(C.Used,'0')) > B.DebLTotal

  )

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM - Scopes and working with API
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM provides API to perform actions such as read or create various objects - Projects, Tasks, Defects etc. Visit SAP Accelerator Business Hub and search for 'SAP Cloud ALM' to get to individual services provided by the API.

What can be sometimes frustrating is when you encounter authentication or authorization issues. This blog post should help you understand the official SAP documentation and give you a small example on how to overcome these obstacles.

Let's say we want to read and create Tasks in SAP Cloud ALM. Then we follow this documentation on the SAP Accelerator Business Hub and we can see we have GET / POST / PATCH / DELETE options.

This documentation on SAP Help will tell us how to construct the URL:
https://<customer-tenant>.<region>.alm.cloud.sap/api/calm-tasks/v1/tasks 

And finally, this documentation on SAP Help describes how to set properly authorized credentials for our client.

Equipped with this information, let's get to it!
In BTP Cockpit, locate to your SAP Cloud ALM subbaccount and in Instances and Subscriptions select 'Create'

We are creating a new Instance. For the demonstration purpose, we create an Instance to work with Tasks only. We name it sap-cloud-alm-create-task.

The most important part - we need to authorize this Instance for a restricted scope of activities. The list of all available API scopes is listed here. We are authorizing the Instance to read and write Tasks. Don't forget to fill the 'xsappname' attribut the same as the Instance name!

Create your Instance and now we need to assign Service Bindings, which is the actual credentials. Let's create one. We will call it postman-call and we will use these credentials to call SAP Cloud ALM from Postman.

Don't fill any json and continue with 'Create'.

 

OAuth2 credentials were generated for us. Now go to Postman and use these credentials. Select OAuth 2.0 as the Auth Type and configure the token mechanism.

The token which we will obtain is authorized as per the scope we set up in the Instance configuration. We can try various operations to ensure that we are able to create a Task, but not able to view a Project.

Hopefully this short guide will help you to put together the pieces. The functionality is well documented, but may be a little scattered around various places.

entry.title: #SITRIO2025 Clean Core para Funcionais - Como proceder?
entry.content:
Palestrante

João Seco é arquiteto de soluções SAP SD na empresa NUMEN, cofundador da plataforma educacional Movie, e organizador do SAP Inside Track São Paulo. Ele se destaca pela sua atuação em conteúdos funcionais, lives sobre SAP, e foco prático em Clean Core, DRC e Reforma Tributária.

Título da Palestra

Clean Core para Funcionais - Como proceder?

Descrição da Palestra

João Seco apresenta de forma descontraída e técnica os fundamentos e aplicações do conceito Clean Core no contexto da SAP S/4HANA. A palestra aborda desde a evolução histórica das customizações no ECC até a importância estratégica do Clean Core para atualizações, performance e escalabilidade nos sistemas modernos SAP.

Resumo

  • Conceito de Clean Core e Evolução SAP
    João começa contextualizando a evolução do SAP ECC para o S/4HANA, ressaltando como customizações excessivas (os famosos "Z") dificultaram atualizações e manutenções. O Clean Core surge como proposta para manter o core do sistema limpo, favorecendo upgrades mais fáceis e soluções escaláveis.

  • Experiências Reais e Casos de Uso
    Com exemplos práticos como a Natura e outras empresas fictícias como "Pargatas", João ilustra como os legados de customizações dificultam a adoção de novas funcionalidades do SAP. A pizza de muçarela representa o SAP standard; a pizza cheia de ingredientes simboliza o sistema sobrecarregado por customizações.

  • Five Golden Rules e Clean Core
    Antes do Clean Core, a SAP já tentava impor limites com as “Five Golden Rules”. Apesar de pouco difundidas, essas regras orientavam sobre como fazer alterações sustentáveis. O Clean Core evolui essas ideias com diretrizes mais claras e integração com o SAP BTP.

  • RICEFW no Mundo Clean Core
    Ele aborda como objetos RICEFW (Reports, Interfaces, Conversions, Enhancements, Forms, Workflows) devem ser tratados no S/4HANA. Ressalta a importância de manter o desenvolvimento no local correto, respeitando o modelo Clean Core, e discute as opções para workflows, como o uso de SAP Build Process Automation.

  • Clean Core vs. Custo-Benefício
    Nem sempre a melhor solução tecnicamente é viável financeiramente. João utiliza analogias urbanas (como construir metrôs caros para poucos usuários) para ilustrar como às vezes um "Z" bem feito é mais sensato que uma integração cara, dependendo do ROI e contexto do cliente.

  • Críticas Construtivas e Visão Prática
    João não é fanático por soluções SAP: reconhece limites e defende escolhas pragmáticas. Defende que Clean Core é sobre “onde desenvolver” e não “não desenvolver”. Ele valoriza soluções padrão, mas admite que personalizações fazem parte da realidade de muitas empresas.

se você deseja ser notificado sobre os próximos eventos SAP Inside Track no Brasil, entre na nossa lista de interesse: Cadastre-se aqui para ser notificado

entry.title: How to post a PO condition value to inventory, GR/IR and a separate G/L?
entry.content:
We wish to add tariff charges to our PO line items so have created a new condition type ZTF1 to do this. Some of its settings are:

Condition class - Discount

Calculation type - Percentage

Condition category - blank

Plus/minus - A (positive)

Accruals - blank

Upon MIGO, the additional tariff charge has been correctly accounted for.

1. DR Inventory - this is correct as we want to the tariff charge to be absorbed into the material cost.

2. CR GR/IR - this is correct as the tariff charge has to be paid to the supplier of the goods.

So far, so good.

However, Finance have created a new G/L account to track all the tariff charges purely for reporting purposes. How can I extend the current setup so that the new tariff G/L will also be populated?

entry.title: #SITRIO2025 Pagamento com cartões integrado ao SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Nome: Luciano Casado
Biografia: Especialista em soluções SAP, Luciano Casado atua com foco em integração de meios de pagamento e arquitetura técnica de sistemas empresariais com o SAP S/4HANA. Com ampla experiência em projetos SAP de grande porte, é conhecido por sua abordagem clara e prática na condução de implementações e discussões técnicas.

Título da Palestra

Pagamento com Cartões Integrado ao SAP S/4HANA

Descrição da Palestra

A palestra de Luciano Casado abordou a complexa integração de sistemas de pagamento com cartão dentro da arquitetura SAP S/4HANA. Ele apresentou as tecnologias disponíveis, os desafios comuns na implementação e como estruturar uma solução escalável, segura e alinhada ao conceito de "Clean Core", adotado como referência pela SAP em projetos modernos.

Resumo

  • Conceito e Evolução do Clean Core
    Luciano contextualizou a origem do conceito "Clean Core", explicando que surgiu como resposta aos desafios de atualização dos sistemas ECC altamente customizados com códigos Z. A palestra mostrou como o Clean Core busca preservar a integridade do código standard, facilitando atualizações e reduzindo custos de manutenção.

  • Comparações com o Modelo Antigo (ECC)
    O palestrante usou metáforas como “pizza de mussarela vs pizza portuguesa” para explicar a evolução dos sistemas ECC para S/4HANA. Ele detalhou como os sistemas antes eram adaptados por desenvolvimentos próprios (Z), enquanto o S/4HANA propõe uso de extensões baseadas em APIs e camadas externas.

  • Casos Reais de Projeto e Implementações
    Foram compartilhadas experiências reais de implementação com empresas como Natura, Alpargatas e Petrobras. Luciano abordou como a cultura organizacional e o nível de maturidade técnica influenciam na adoção do modelo Clean Core.

  • Desenvolvimento: Quando usar Clean Core e quando não usar
    A palestra apresentou os diferentes tipos de desenvolvimento (RICEFW) e orientações sobre quando é mais vantajoso seguir o padrão Clean Core ou adotar soluções Z. A decisão deve considerar custo, ROI e criticidade da função para o negócio.

  • APIs e Integrações com Sistemas de Pagamento
    Luciano explorou como a integração com cartões de pagamento pode ser feita via APIs no S/4HANA, destacando as boas práticas para manter a escalabilidade, auditabilidade e compliance. Destacou-se o uso de camadas intermediárias para acoplamento com gateways de pagamento.

  • Workflow e Automação de Processos
    Ele criticou o uso de workflows Z e sugeriu como alternativa o uso da solução SAP Build Process Automation via BTP, promovendo soluções “clean” que respeitam o core SAP sem alterar os padrões.

  • Relatórios e Visualizações
    A geração de relatórios foi outro ponto analisado. Foi enfatizado o uso de CDS Views e Fiori como ferramentas limpas para exposição de dados e dashboards, em vez de ALVs customizados tradicionais.

  • Mensagens Finais e Reflexão Estratégica
    Luciano reforçou que mais do que aplicar soluções tecnológicas, é fundamental entender o impacto da arquitetura no futuro dos negócios, incluindo atualizações futuras do SAP e escalabilidade de processos.

se você deseja ser notificado sobre os próximos eventos SAP Inside Track no Brasil, entre na nossa lista de interesse: Cadastre-se aqui para ser notificado

entry.title: #SITRIO2025 Painel de Perguntas & Respostas: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
• @Renan_Correa: Consultor com forte atuação em integrações fiscais com SAP S/4HANA e Cloud Platform Integration.

  • @Secco: Especialista em arquitetura de soluções SAP com foco em extensibilidade e clean core.

  • @Marssel700 : Reconhecido profissional SAP com ampla experiência prática e forte envolvimento na comunidade.

  • @fabiodezan : Referência em implementações de soluções fiscais SAP no Brasil, com ênfase na aplicabilidade real do Public Cloud.

Descrição
O painel foi um espaço interativo para esclarecimento de dúvidas e compartilhamento de experiências práticas sobre o uso da SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, especialmente nos aspectos fiscais, integrações com parceiros, desafios de contabilização e estratégias de mitigação de "vendor lock-in". A conversa revelou nuances críticas da adaptação do ERP à realidade fiscal brasileira.

Introdução

Durante o SAP Inside Track Rio de Janeiro 2025, um dos momentos mais ricos do evento foi o painel de perguntas e respostas com os especialistas Renan, João Secco, Marssel e Dezan. O debate aprofundou-se nas práticas, desafios e soluções fiscais na implementação do SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, destacando aspectos de integração com parceiros fiscais, contabilização e flexibilidade arquitetural.

Panorama da Integração Fiscal no S/4HANA

A discussão iniciou-se com os desafios enfrentados na aplicação de legislação fiscal brasileira, altamente complexa. Ferramentas como a Sovos permitem rastreabilidade do cálculo fiscal e sua justificativa, fornecendo logs que embasam decisões automáticas. A arquitetura cloud requer atenção especial para tratar cenários como Repetro, notas complementares e entrega futura.

Flexibilidade e Extensibilidade

Foi evidenciado que, enquanto o Public Cloud possui limitações de extensibilidade, é possível contornar restrições via CPI e padrões de payload. Já no Private Cloud ou On-Premise, é possível utilizar BAdIs para ajustar comportamento de cálculo e retorno fiscal.

Contabilização e Responsabilidade Fiscal

A SAP entrega pacotes de contabilização padronizados, mas a responsabilidade de contabilizar corretamente é do cliente. No Public Cloud, a flexibilização é menor, sendo essencial alinhar condições de cálculo e escrituração diretamente com os dados fornecidos pelos parceiros.

Evitando o Vendor Lock-in

Uma preocupação comum é o aprisionamento tecnológico com fornecedores fiscais. A solução apontada passa pela padronização dos payloads e arquitetura modular, permitindo a troca de parceiros sem grandes reestruturações. Foi citada a possibilidade de operar cenários híbridos com múltiplos parceiros simultâneos.

Impacto de Dados Incorretos

Uma questão-chave abordada foi a responsabilização por cálculos errados devido a dados mal formatados. Os especialistas foram unânimes em afirmar que, independentemente de quem realiza o cálculo, a responsabilidade pelo envio correto dos dados é do cliente SAP.

Cálculos em Massa e Personalização de Preços

Foi confirmada a viabilidade de realizar cálculos em massa via APIs para exibir ao cliente final o valor com impostos incluídos. Essa funcionalidade é vital para setores como o farmacêutico, onde regras de desconto afetam diretamente a tributação.

Conclusão

O painel ofereceu uma visão completa e prática sobre os dilemas fiscais do S/4HANA Public Cloud. Ficou claro que, com arquitetura adequada, padronização e bom alinhamento com os parceiros fiscais, é possível implementar soluções robustas e responsivas à realidade brasileira.

Este evento é um exemplo de como o compartilhamento de experiências práticas pode acelerar o aprendizado e evitar erros críticos em projetos SAP.

entry.title: Issue When Deleting Promotions in Executive Review After Recent Release – Blocking Error on Calculat
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

we are currently experiencing an issue in our Compensation Executive Review worksheets after the latest release and would like to ask if anyone else has encountered this behavior or can share insights.

  • We have promotions that were entered before the new release.

  • After the release, the step owner in Executive Review tried to delete a promotion using the three-dot menu > Promote > Delete.

  • Upon doing so, a pop-up error appears (see attached screenshot), and after confirming, the worksheet cannot be saved anymore.

  • A red flag appears next to the employee name, and a calculated field turns red.

  • The error pop-up also occurs when adding or deleting a promotion for the first time after the release.

  • However, the blocking behavior only happens in Executive Review.

  • In individual worksheets, the error popup appears but doesn’t prevent saving or trigger a red error.

We were able to fix individual cases by:

  • Re-inserting and then re-deleting the promotion directly in the individual worksheet (not via Executive Review).

  • This also updated the Executive Review view and removed the red flag and the calculated field error.

  • Could this be an Executive Review-specific issue introduced by the new release?

  • Would running the "Update All Worksheets" job help sync Executive Review and individual worksheets and prevent this from happening again?

  • Any known issues with calculated fields and promotions logic in Executive Review after the recent release?

Thanks in advance for any help or suggestions!

entry.title: #SITRIO2025 SAP Analytics Cloud: Soluções Inteligentes para Negócios Orientados por Dados
entry.content:
Nome do Palestrante:
@Jonys_Arcanjo 

Biografia do Palestrante:
Jonys Arcanjo é especialista em soluções SAP com vasta experiência em integração e análise de dados corporativos. Atua em projetos de transformação digital utilizando SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) e SAP Analytics Cloud.

Título da Palestra:
SAP Analytics Cloud: Soluções Inteligentes para Negócios Orientados por Dados

Descrição da Palestra:
Jonys apresentou um panorama sobre os desafios enfrentados na integração de eventos fiscais via SAP, discutindo práticas recomendadas, limitações atuais e perspectivas futuras, com ênfase no uso de SAP Analytics Cloud para fornecer inteligência de negócios.

Introdução

A transformação digital tem acelerado a necessidade de soluções analíticas inteligentes nas organizações. A palestra de Jonys Arcanjo no SAP Inside Track Rio de Janeiro 2025 oferece uma visão detalhada sobre os desafios e soluções envolvidas na utilização do SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) e sua integração com ferramentas fiscais como o GRC (Governance, Risk and Compliance) e DRC (Document and Reporting Compliance).

Desafios com Eventos Fiscais e a Integração com SAP

Jonys apontou as dificuldades recorrentes na interação com a Central de Autorização de Faturas (CFaz), como o download excessivo de eventos fiscais não necessários e o comportamento inconsistente do sistema ao apresentar esses eventos. Esse problema implica em cobranças desnecessárias e maior complexidade na gestão de dados fiscais.

Uso Simultâneo de GRC e DRC

Uma das abordagens práticas apresentadas foi o uso simultâneo do GRC para dados históricos enquanto o DRC é utilizado para novos eventos. Essa prática, embora funcional, exige um enriquecimento da interface de exibição de notas fiscais antigas, pois a RFC migrada não acessa o GRC diretamente.

Importância do SAP Analytics Cloud

O SAC entra como uma ferramenta essencial para transformar esses dados brutos em insights de negócio. Ele permite visualizações dinâmicas, relatórios inteligentes e tomada de decisão baseada em dados em tempo real, superando as limitações dos sistemas operacionais legados.

Conclusão

A palestra encerrou de forma descontraída, com sorteios de livros e uma foto coletiva entre os participantes, reforçando o espírito colaborativo do evento. Mais do que apresentar problemas, Jonys Arcanjo propôs caminhos reais e acessíveis para empresas que desejam evoluir na sua jornada de dados com o SAP Analytics Cloud.

entry.title: #SITRIO2025 Inovações nas interfaces bancarias
entry.content:
Palestrante: Manuel Robalinho @MRobalinho 
Biografia: Especialista em tecnologias SAP, com ampla experiência em arquitetura de soluções fiscais e financeiras para o setor bancário. Atuando como consultor e líder técnico, é reconhecido por sua atuação em projetos de alto impacto e inovação em SAP no Brasil.
Título da Palestra: Inovações nas Interfaces Bancárias
Descrição da Palestra: A apresentação de Manuel Robalinho aborda os desafios e avanços tecnológicos na integração das interfaces bancárias com soluções SAP, destacando o papel dos parceiros fiscais, arquitetura técnica, escalabilidade, inteligência embutida e potencial de adaptação às legislações brasileiras complexas.

Resumo

  • Transparência e Rastreabilidade: Robalinho explica como soluções como a Sovos oferecem logs detalhados e tracing para justificar legislações aplicadas em transações. Isso permite que empresas auditam decisões fiscais com base técnica.

  • Integração Técnica com SAP: O palestrante detalha como o SAP pode ser configurado para tratar operações especiais (como Repetro ou Nota Complementar), destacando a flexibilidade das BADIs no ambiente private cloud, que permitem personalização no envio e retorno dos cálculos fiscais.

  • Interoperabilidade entre Parceiros: Uma questão crítica abordada foi a troca de parceiros fiscais. Robalinho explicou que isso é tecnicamente simples, bastando redirecionar o endpoint. Cenários híbridos são possíveis, onde múltiplos parceiros são usados para diferentes tipos de operação.

  • Padronização e Independência: A padronização dos payloads SAP permite que múltiplos parceiros integrem-se sem alterar a estrutura básica de comunicação. Assim, a empresa mantém independência tecnológica, reduzindo vendor lock-in.

  • Responsabilidade Fiscal: Foi enfatizado que o motor de cálculo atua com base nas informações fornecidas pelo SAP, portanto, erros de cadastro impactam diretamente os resultados. A responsabilidade pelos cálculos continua sendo da empresa usuária.

  • Cálculo Antecipado de Impostos no e-Commerce: Um ponto inovador foi a possibilidade de utilizar o motor de cálculo fora do SAP, como em um site de e-commerce, para gerar o preço final com base em regras fiscais. Isso permite exibir preços corretos com impostos dinâmicos em tempo real.

  • Modernização com APIs e Automação: Mostrou como empresas podem consumir as APIs de cálculo fiscal de qualquer ponto — SAP, site, ABAP — e realizar cálculos em massa ou sequenciais. Isso gera escalabilidade e integração com múltiplas plataformas.

  • Desativação de Componentes Antigos: A apresentação fecha destacando a desativação de certos grupos de conditions (como BX e BIC) com a adoção de parceiros fiscais externos, que já devolvem os resultados prontos, simplificando a contabilização e escrituração no SAP.

entry.title: #SITRIO2025 Apresentação da Comunidade SAP no Rio de Janeiro
entry.content:
Nome do Palestrante:

Marssel Vilaça @Marssel700 

Bio do Palestrante:
Marssel Vilaça é um dos mais antigos participantes ativos da comunidade SAP, com envolvimento desde 2007. Atualmente integra o programa internacional de influenciadores SAP Champions. Atua como elo entre a SAP Global e o cenário brasileiro, promovendo eventos, disseminando conteúdos estratégicos e fomentando a interação entre usuários e tecnologias emergentes como IA e BTP.

Título da Palestra:
"O Poder da Comunidade SAP e a Força do Networking Local"

Descrição Breve da Palestra:
Nesta palestra introdutória ao SAP Inside Track Rio 2025, Marssel Vilaça apresenta a história, os valores e a estrutura da comunidade SAP. Ele explora como eventos como esse fortalecem conexões profissionais e técnicas, destacando o papel fundamental das plataformas digitais e da colaboração entre pares para a evolução das soluções SAP.

Resumo Educativo

  • Abertura com reconhecimento da comunidade:
    Marssel começa destacando a importância do engajamento dos participantes que escolheram estar presentes num sábado, e reforça que a agenda do evento é uma das melhores dos últimos anos.

  • Histórico e papel da SAP Community:
    Desde 2007, Marssel é ativo na comunidade SAP, que se consolidou como a maior plataforma de compartilhamento de conhecimento sobre SAP. A plataforma inclui fóruns de perguntas (questions), blogs técnicos e eventos locais — como o próprio Inside Track.

  • Objetivo dos eventos Inside Track:
    Trata-se de encontros presenciais, não oficiais da SAP, mas com seu apoio, voltados para troca de experiências entre profissionais. Marssel reforça que eles são organizados localmente e focam sempre em trazer conteúdos novos, não repetidos de eventos anteriores.

  • Vantagens do networking presencial:
    Ele enfatiza o valor das conexões humanas, da troca informal de experiências e reencontros com colegas de projetos antigos. O ambiente amigável e colaborativo é visto como diferencial fundamental.

  • Importância dos blogs na comunidade SAP:
    Segundo Marssel, blogs funcionam como “mini-palestras escritas”, que servem tanto para compartilhar soluções quanto para criar um portfólio profissional. Ele mesmo escreve e armazena conteúdos úteis que utiliza em interações com colegas.

  • Exemplo de evolução local da comunidade:
    Rio de Janeiro agora conta com uma landing page oficial dentro da comunidade SAP, centralizando todos os conteúdos, eventos e blogs regionais — um grande avanço em relação a iniciativas do passado que foram descontinuadas.

  • Chamada à ação:
    Ao final, Marssel convida os presentes a escanearem um QR Code para ingressarem no grupo oficial da comunidade SAP Rio de Janeiro, reforçando o compromisso com atualizações e integração local.

se você deseja ser notificado sobre os próximos eventos SAP Inside Track no Brasil, entre na nossa lista de interesse: Cadastre-se aqui para ser notificado

entry.title: #SITRIO2025 Abertura do SAP Inside Track Rio de Janeiro 2025
entry.content:
A abertura conduzida por Eduardo Palafoz e outros colaboradores apresenta os voluntários, os objetivos do evento, o retorno ao formato presencial e a proposta de promover conexão entre profissionais e troca de conhecimento técnico dentro da comunidade SAP.

 

  • Recepção acolhedora e identidade local:
    A abertura do evento foi marcada por trilhas sonoras que evocam o espírito carioca e uma introdução descontraída que celebrou o reencontro da comunidade SAP.

  • Apresentação da equipe organizadora:
    Diversos voluntários se apresentaram, como Adriana Brito, Milena Boniolo, Carlos Limbert, Michele Machado, Roberto Fernandes e Leonardo. Eles destacaram o esforço para realizar o evento e incentivaram os participantes a interagir e aproveitar ao máximo.

  • Fala de Eduardo Palafoz:
    Eduardo explicou seu papel no apoio à organização, reforçando a relevância do evento como espaço de aprendizado e confraternização. Salientou que o encontro estava previsto para durar até as 18h com diversas atividades programadas.

  • Contextualização de Marssel Vilaça:
    @Marssel700 destacou a relevância do evento e sua qualidade, comparando-o favoravelmente com outras edições anteriores em todo o Brasil. Destacou o papel da comunidade SAP e sua conexão com a SAP Internacional, além de sua atuação como SAP Champion.

  • Importância do networking e comunidade:
    Reforçada a proposta do evento como espaço para reconexão de amigos e colegas de projetos anteriores, fortalecendo a rede profissional. Enfatizado também o papel caloroso da comunidade carioca.

  • Histórico e evolução dos SAP Inside Tracks:
    Apresentado o surgimento da iniciativa como esforço comunitário global, com apoio informal da SAP. Hoje, o SAP Inside Track acontece em mais de 50 cidades, com autonomia local e foco em compartilhamento de experiências práticas.

  • Relação com a SAP Community Platform:
    A nova plataforma oferece um espaço digital para participantes do Rio de Janeiro se manterem atualizados com conteúdos, blogs e novos eventos. Relembrado que houve um hiato de anos, e agora o grupo retorna com foco específico por localidade.

  • Encerramento e introdução à próxima palestra:
    Encerrada a abertura com o anúncio de que João Seco conduziria a palestra seguinte sobre Clean Core. Foi criada uma expectativa positiva e animada pela contribuição do palestrante.

entry.title: How SAP Digital Manufacturing Simplifies Tool Management
entry.content:
Managing tools on the shop floor shouldn’t slow down your production.

With SAP Digital Manufacturing's Tools Scheduling feature, manufacturers get a simple, powerful way to make sure the right tools are in the right place, at the right time. This capability helps teams plan ahead, avoid conflicts, and keep operations running smoothly.

It’s all about using your tools better, reducing delays, and improving reliability in your production process.

Here are five key benefits of Tools Scheduling and Dispatching in SAP Digital Manufacturing:

🔧Efficient Resource Utilization
Enables reservation and assignment of production tools to specific work centers and resources, ensuring optimal use of available assets.

📅Improved Planning Accuracy
Provides a calendar-based view and downloadable reports on tool assignments, aiding in better planning and forecast visibility.

⚠️Conflict Avoidance
Identifies overlapping tool assignments, allowing supervisors to resolve conflicts before execution begins.

📈Integrated with Dispatching
Tool assignments are considered during dispatching, ensuring alignment of resources and tool availability with production schedules.

📊Enhanced Traceability and Reporting
Generates comprehensive reports for tool usage linked to production operations, supporting auditability and continuous improvement efforts.

Learn more about it at: Tool Management with SAP Digital Manufacturing

Do you like this post? Please let me know in the comments section what you think. Any feedback is highly appreciated.
Or, if you have any questions, please check SAP Community Q&A Area, or comment down below.

Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Joule–SuccessFactors: Setup Challenges and Resolutions
entry.content:
Let's look at the common issues that we may have while setting up Joule with SuccessFactors:

  1. Joule does not work in the SFSF account
  2. Joule gives me the option to select Default Login and IAS Login 
  3. Joule gives me a Blank screen. In case you have an Azure AD/OKTA and Cloud Identity is a Proxy System
  4. LPS_SFSF_dt destination check connections fail with 401: Unauthorized
  5. I see the Joule chat history in another/colleague's system
  6. The Joule Navigation button is missing after a successful Jobrun or Jobsync
  7. CDM does now allow users to launch the site manager – channel manager and gives an error
  8. IPS – creating target system we do not see an option for Workzone Std, edition
  9. How to find out if you have SCIM / oData URL in your IPS Source file and how to change it
  10. Quick Setup Use the “Source System and Target System” .json files
  11. Joule authentication is not working when the browser '3rd-party cookie blocking policy' is enabled
  12. How do I verify if all groups are created for Joule
  13. I am using my new Common Super Domain (CSD) with my SFSF and Joule URL validation fails during Booster
  14. I am unable to use Joule in Incognito mode - Chrome Browser.
  15. Booster execution fails with an error
  16. "Oops, something went wrong. How about trying something different?" - Joule does not work.
  17. Joule in SAP SuccessFactors - View Phone Number Behavior
  18. Processing Error - WebSocket Error in Inspect - Network Tab

    ==================================================================================

    So let us take a look at the issue and how to fix them:

    1. Joule does not work in the SFSF account
       

       

       

      Reason: This issue is related to Trusted Domains in your setup.

      Fix: Please go back to the setup blog and refer to the section 

      2.1 Configure Trusted Domains for SAP Authorization and Trust Management Service

      4. Adding Trusted Domains and Configure Assertion Attributes in SAP Cloud Identity Services (CIS)

       

      2. Joule gives me the option to select Default Login and IAS Login 

      Reason: you have both Default Logon and IAS available for User Login in your BTP Subaccount

      Fix: Log in to your SAP BTP Cockpit -> Navigate to your Joule Subaccount -> Expand Security option -> Click on Trust Configuration -> Click on Edit option for the Default Identity Provider (sap.default) -> Remove the tick mark for Available for User Logon and save the settings.

       

       

       

      Once the settings are saved, you may clear the cookies and try to log in.

       

      3. Joule gives me a Blank screen. In case you have an Azure AD/OKTA and Cloud Identity is a Proxy System, please follow this. 

      Issue: In most cases, your SSO should be taken care and Joule should be able to log you in with SFSF.  In case you have a login screen with Joule as shown below and if you are using Cloud Identity as a proxy, you may want to configure additional settings.

       

       

      Or A Blank Screen as below:

       

       

       

      Fix:

      • Go to your SAP Cloud Identity Services -> click on Identity Providers -> click Corporate Identity Providers -> select your provider Azure AD/OKTA that is configured -> Ensure your SSO Forward All SSO Requests to Corporate IDP turned Off.
      • Now click on Application & Resources -> click on Applications -> click on your BTP Joule Application -> click on Conditional Authentication option -> and change the Default Authentication Identity Provider to your Azure AD/OKTA and save the settings.

         

         

         

         

        4. LPS_SFSF_dt destination check connections fail with 401: Unauthorized

        Issue: This could happen if you have the URL incorrect or your User and Password are incorrect.

         

         

        Fix:

        • Please ensure your SFSF URL is correct and you can refer to the SAP Note: 2215682 - SuccessFactors API URLs and external Ips
        • Check for Internal IP Restrictions that might be applied. You may refer to this link for Regions and API Endpoints Available for the Cloud Foundry Environment
        • The next option is to Reset your password
        • If the issue continues, please check the URL in the Postman client for possible reasons could be the wrong URL picked up

          Best Practise: Create a Technical User that is not used by any user and set the password to never expire in your SuccessFactors system.

           

          5. I see the Joule chat history in another/colleague's system

          Issue: Users can see the Joule conversations in multiple logins with different systems although the login details are different.

          Reason: In most of the SFSF Dev / Preview systems, customers have a dummy email created which is common for all users with actual employee details. This can be due to multiple reasons as they take a copy of production to the Dev / Preview system. A dummy email address is created to avoid sending emails to actual users from the Dev / Preview system.

          Eg: sap@dummy.com, dummy@dummy.com

          Fix: In case you have the dummy emails configured for all the users, in your Dev/ Preview system, you may try the following options:

          • Change the Developers / Users email address in the Dev / Preview system to the actual user ID to fix the issues. Remember if you do an IPS sync from your production system, the emails will return to dummy emails as per your settings
          • Change the Subject Name Identifier -> Basic Configurations in your Cloud Identity Services from Email Address to Login Name (This change is to do with your Application of Joule).  Remember once this change is done, users have to use the Login Name.

            Once the above changes are done, you may also need to add the Launchpad_Admin User with the Login Name to your SAP BTP Subaccount. In case you already have the user created with an email address, delete it, create a new one with a Login Name, and assign the roles.   

             

             

            6. The Joule Navigation button is missing after a successful Jobrun or Jobsync

            Ans: While most of the Setup is complete and jobs have been executed as required if the navigation button is missing (refer to the image for the navigation icon), you will have to check the NavigationService settings at your subaccount destinations.

             

             

            Navigate to your subaccount -> Click on Connectivity -> Click on Destinations -> Select NavigationService -> Click on Export.

             

             

             

             

            Open the file that you have exported in a notepad and ensure the value of tokenServiceULRType is Dedicated. In case you see the value as Common, go back to your BTP Cockpit, edit the Navigation service select Dedicated, and save the settings. Export the settings and validate the saved changes.

             

             

            In case the issue continues, a manual refresh of the Content Channel in the Workzone instance is required to make sure Service provider details are updated correctly.

            Go to the Work Zone service instance in the BTP subaccount, select Content Channel, choose the service provider connected to SF, click Report, and verify whether Role is assigned correctly. In the below screenshot, the Role is empty.

             


             

             

            Go back to your Channel Manager screen and click on the Refresh button to update the Role to the provider that you have created.

             

             

            Wait for the refresh to complete, once it’s done, click Report to view the details.

             

             

            If you see the 19/19 in the Role section, we are good. Please log off your SAP SuccessFactors wait for 30 mins for the background jobs to execute and then try again.

             

            7. CDM does now allow users to launch the site manager – channel manager and gives an error.

            Issue: while launching the admin user from SFSF to the workzone, if the users are not synchronized it may give an error as below.

             

             
             

             

            Reason: user sync is not complete yet.

            Fix:

            • Ensure the correct role is assigned to your user with a custom identity service
            • Access the Launchpad_Admin role & navigate to Users  to add the user for example:
            •  

            •  

              • Run the job in the admin center - > Job Scheduler tab- > job Type ( Refresh Synthetic Group Data) and click on Run it Now.

                 

                 


                 

                 

                8. IPS – creating target system we do not see an option for Workzone Std, edition

                Issue: The customer has IAS and IPS tenants with separate tenants and not in a common tenant and while creating the target system, the workzone does not show up.

                 

                 

                Reason: The IPS landscape is on SAP NEO and the service needs to be upgraded to multi-cloud.

                Fix: 

                They can upgrade it using the help guide or refer to the blog -

                • https://help.sap.com/docs/identity-provisioning/identity-provisioning-internal/migrate-identity-prov...
                • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/go-for-your-quick-win-migrate-identity-provisio...

                  Important - The ideal upgrade takes anywhere from 1 hour to 1 day depending on the complexity of the IPS setup from the customer. Please ensure to check the status of the Source/Target/Jobs that were scheduled.

                   

                  9. How to find out if you have SCIM / oData URL in your IPS Source file and how to change it

                  Issue: Workzone Groups are supported with SCIM2.0 and the oData API version is not recommended.

                  How to check: you can log in to your SAP Cloud Identity Services, navigate to your Source System of your SFSF -> Properties, and look for the URL as shown below.


                  Fix: Make a copy of your Source System and change the values according to the Joule Setup or use the Source & Target Files attached to this blog (bottom). The how to add the Source and Target details are shared in the next step.  

                  Note: If you are downloading the Source & Target Files attached to this blog, please change the file extension from .txt to .json before importing to your Cloud Identity Services. Check step 10 for setup.

                   

                  10. Quick Setup Use the “Source System and Target System” .json files

                  In my previous blog on Joule setup, we had discussed setup using the existing SFSF Source, here we are creating a new Source System in both cases either your URL is using oData and/or a new Joule setup. The new Source System will help us to keep the Joule setup separate and not make changes to your existing setup of your SFSF.

                  Adding Source System:

                  • Navigate to your SAP Cloud Identity Services – Navigate to Source System -> click on Add -> Import the file “SuccessFactors - SF-Company-ID - Joule.json” and edit the System Name to your SFSF Company ID as shown below and feel free to add a Description and save the settings.

                      

                     

                    • The required “Transformation” changes are taken care of in the JSON file which is attached to the blog, so we can directly Navigate to your Properties Tab and change the values as per the Joule Setup as shown below

                       

                      After the changes, my source looks like this:


                        

                       

                      • Since we have added this as a new Source, we should also be exporting the Certificates and important to your SuccessFactors system. Click on Outbound Certificate, and click on Download the certificate to make a copy of the new source system that you have created.

                          

                         

                        • Go to your SuccessFactors system, go to Security Center – click on X.509 Public Certificate Mapping, and click on Add, Change the Configuration Name as per your requirement, Change the Integration Name to Identity Provisioning Service, and select the new certificate that you downloaded and Save the settings.  

                          Note: Do not enter any details in “Login Name”, it should be blank.


                           

                          Adding Target System:

                          Now let us add the Target System using the JSON file, Navigate to your Target System, click on Add, and select the file “WorkZone_Target_ForJoule.json”, once the file is added, you need to change the System Name and add a Description to recognize your setup. Ensure to Select the Source System that was saved in the previous step before you navigate to the Properties Tab.


                            

                           

                           

                          You may not be able to save until you enter the details required in the Properties tab. Navigate to the Properties tab, and enter the details from your ServiceKey file downloaded from your Subaccount - SAP Workzone ServiceKey. Once you enter the details, you should be able to proceed with your Job Sync.   


                            

                           

                           

                          Note: Since we are using the Certificate for authentication, please use this link to copy your URL based on your data center - mTLS Certificate Server. Example: https://api55preview.cert.sapsf.eu

                          https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_PLATFORM/d599f15995d348a1b45ba5603e2aba9b/af2b8d5437494....

                          11. Joule authentication not working when the browser '3rd-party cookie blocking policy' is enabled

                          Issue: Joule may not be able to work while 3rd party cookie is blocked. This may happen mostly in incognito or in private mode.

                          Fix: Please look at the SAP Note: 3428564 - Joule authentication not working when browser '3rd-party cookie blocking policy' is enable...

                           

                          12. How do I verify if all groups are created for Joule

                          Issue: in case of setup issues, Jobs may not run properly and we need to validate the Groups created/assigned to a user.

                          Fix: Based on your Job Logs from your Cloud Identity Services, you may log in to your SAP Workzone, Navigate to your SAP Joule Subaccount, click on Instances and Subscriptions -> click on the SAP Workzone, Standard Edition (ensure you have the Role “Launchpad_Admin” is assigned to you), click on the Settings Tab and enter the User Email or the Global User ID to check the assigned Roles.


                            

                           

                           

                          In case you need further analysis, refer to the blog by Harjeet Judge on - Leverage SCIM APIs of SAP Build Work Zone to view users and groups provisioned into Work Zone

                           

                          13. I am using my new Common Super Domain (CSD) with my SFSF and Joule URL validation fails during Booster.

                          Issue: If you are using the CSD then your SFSF URL would have migrated to a new one and Joule fails to validate the New URL

                          Fix: You can look at the CSD Migration Customer/Partner Guide to match your SFSF Admin URL to successfully run the booster. Refer to Chapter 5 for the URL details.

                           

                          14. I am unable to use Joule in Incognito mode - Chrome Browser

                           Reason: Third-party cookies could have been blocked on your Browser

                           Solution: turn off the Third-party cookies and try to log in.


                            

                           

                           

                          15. Booster execution fails with an error

                           

                          Reason: One of the reasons could be that you have not established trust in your sub-account with the Cloud Identity Services. 

                           

                           

                          Fix: Before you run the Joule Booster, please follow 2. Configure SAP Cloud Identity Services(CIS)in our setup blog - (you need to establish Trust for your subaccount and cloud identity services) as this has been added as a prerequisite to the new update to the Booster.

                          16. "Oops, something went wrong. How about trying something different?" - Joule does not work.

                          Reason: This could be due to multiple scenarios due to GUID Mismatch, IP Restrictions, or Login and Password Policies IP for individual users.

                          Case 0: GUID Mismatch Fix: You can refer to the KBA - https://me.sap.com/notes/0003488269 if this is not fixed, please check the following Case 1 and Case 2.

                          Case 1:

                          In your SAP SuccessFactors -> Navigate to IP Restriction Management -> If this page has No data, we are good.

                           

                           

                          In case your company is allowing the whitelisting of certain IPs for internal rules, only then,  you should add the SAP BTP NAT IPs that are related to your Joule Service. Look at the fix listed after Case 2.

                          Case 2:

                          If your company has maintained your users with certain IPs, this issue could happen only for you. To verify this, you can navigate to Password & Login Policy Settings -> Expand to option Set API login exceptions -> If you do not have any User ID listed, we are all good.

                          If you(r)/any User ID is listed with an IP Address, then Joule will respond “Oops, something went wrong.”, to fix this, follow the steps below.

                           

                           

                          Fix(Case 1 and Case 2):

                          To find the SAP BTP Subaccount Data Center, go to your SAP BTP Account -> navigate to your Subaccount where with Joule Services -> in the Overview section you should be able to see the Cloud Foundry Environment, please look at API Endpoint -> In my case its https://api.cf.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com/ so my CF Data Center is “us10”.

                           

                           

                          Once you have your CF Data Center, please go to Regions and API Endpoints Available for the Cloud Foundry Environment, and search for your respective value in the Technical Key as below.

                           

                           

                          Look for the “NAT IPs (egress, IPs for requests from a Cloud Foundry app)” and you need to copy the values related to your CF account. In my case, it's “cf-us10-001”.

                           

                           

                          For Case 1: Copy one IP at a time and create an entry in your SFSF System in IP Restrictions Management.

                           

                           

                          You should re-login once this is done.

                          Caution: In case your company does not have any IP Restrictions and if you add it for BTP only, it may cause issues for Login from other IPs that are not listed in this. Please be cautious before adding this.

                          For Case 2: You can copy the entire IP Address from NAT IPs (egress, IPs for requests from a Cloud Foundry app), edit the User ID in Set API login exceptions -> append the IP to this list, and Save the settings.  

                          Tip: For Joule to function properly, you may need to do this for all the users listed here. If a user is not listed, there is no need to add them.

                          17. Joule in SAP SuccessFactors - View Phone Number Behavior

                          Issue: Phone Number may not respond as expected.

                          Fix: Refer to the KBA - 3458399

                          18. Processing Error - WebSocket Error in Inspect - Network Tab

                          Issue: Unable to use Joule as it gives you Processing Error for all Employees in the organization.

                          Reason: There could be many, but one of the ways to isolate it is using the steps below:

                          - Check-in external laptop or via public internet if Joule works

                          - Is this issue only for you?

                          In case it's for all employees, please do an "inspect" in the browser while launching Joule service and see if you see the below error related to WebSocket:

                           

                           

                           

                          Fix: Contact your Local IT / Network team. They could have blocked your Joule app, considering it's a third-party application. Once they whitelist it, Joule should be working as usual. 

                          Credits to all the team members @harjeetjudge @Nageshcaparthy.

      Thanks,
      Vishnu.cr.

entry.title: New member
entry.content:
Hi

My name is Vítor, I’m from Portugal, and I’m very excited to be part of this space for sharing and professional growth. Over the past few months, I’ve been fully dedicated to completing trainings to consolidate my knowledge and prepare myself.

  • SAP Consultant Career Start: I gained a broad overview of SAP modules, implementation methodologies, and consulting best practices.

  • SAP Financial Consultant: I specialized in financial processes, accounting, and reporting in an SAP environment, with an emphasis on FI-CO integration.

I would love to hear your thoughts on the best paths to start working with SAP.

  • Which resources (blogs, videos, advanced courses) do you recommend for deepening skills?

  • How can I gain practical experience while looking for my first opportunity (e.g., personal projects, volunteering, internships)?

I’d appreciate your suggestions, career-transition stories, and practical advice to help me take my first steps.

Thank you for your help

Vitor

entry.title: Error: "View XYZ can't be assigned since no restriction could be determined"
entry.content:
A Business Role has been configured however, users assigned to this role do not see the requested work center view.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Error: "View XYZ can't be assigned since no restricti...
entry.content:
The restriction rule is based on the user's organizational unit assignment. Depending on the selected restriction rules, the system verifies whether the assigned organization is designated as a Sales or Service Organization.

If the flag is missing, the work center view will not be assigned to the user. Therefore, it is important to ensure that the Business User is assigned to a valid organizational unit.

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite OData V4 receiving format JSON
entry.content: SAP S4 APIs support json, but the OData Adapter in CI don't. That's why you can post from postman, but not through CI. I believe you could change your adapter from Odata to REST then json should work, but of course you would lose the functionalities of odata channel

entry.title: How to include Segment in an organisation-type public dimension hierarchy in SAC with standard API?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am working on building a public dimension of type Organisation in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) to represent an organisational hierarchy.
The hierarchy should reflect the following structure:

Segment → Profit Center Groups → Profit Centers → Cost Centers

So far, I have been using standard SAP APIs to bring the data for Profit Centers and Cost Centers successfully. However, I am facing challenges including the Segment level in the hierarchy.

The Segment field exists in the query behind Profit Centers, but it does not appear or cannot be linked correctly in the public dimension.

My setup uses an import connection to SAC, and the public dimension is based on these standard APIs.

My questions are:

  1. Is it possible to include Segment as a level above Profit Center Groups in a public dimension of type Organisation in SAC?
  2. If yes, how can I properly configure the hierarchy to include Segment?
  3. Are there any recommended SAP APIs or CDS views that support this Segment → Profit Center Groups → Profit Centers → Cost Centers hierarchy out of the box?
  4. If this is not possible directly, what would be the best practice to model this hierarchy with Segment included? Should I create a custom model or custom hierarchy in SAC?

Any guidance, example configurations, or pointers to SAP documentation would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance for your support.

#SAPAnalyticsCloud #PublicDimension #OrganisationHierarchy #Segment #ProfitCenter #CostCenter #SAPAPIs

E.g. Stop light images (red, yellow, green), change a fourth image to red by referencing the name (all images in the same section of report design). Is this possible?

entry.title: Sapphire 2025 guide to sessions and resources on AI in SAP BTP
entry.content:
As you prepare to attend Sapphire 2025, we invite you to explore the exciting world of AI within SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).  

Whether you want to infuse AI into your applications and drive innovation, develop applications more productively with Joule or build custom skills and agents with Joule Studio, here are some resources that will empower you in your journey 

New AI offerings in SAP Build: Read about the AI capabilities available to SAP Build and ABAP developers 

  • AI in SAP BTP: Explore all AI offerings available on SAP BTP 
  • Joule for developers: Unlock new levels of productivity with AI in SAP Build 
  • Joule for developers: Joule speaks ABAP! 
  • Joule for developers: Enable Joule in SAP Build Process Automation & SAP Build Apps 
  • Joule for developers: Frequently asked questions 
  • Whitepaper: ABAP Development in the age of AI 
  • Video- AI in Enterprise Automation - https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/mwr9wEr?rc=10&doi=SAP1190165 
  • Video- AI in Integration Suite - https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/wXfj279?rc=10&doi=SAP1182603 

BTP Interactive learning tools: Get guidance with available SAP resources covering aspects like application development, integration, data analytics, and AI 

  • Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities: Take a product tour 
  • Build Code Test Drive: Experiment without risks  
  • Code Samples: Generative AI hub starter kit  
  • Reference architecture: Optimal setup guides  
  • Step-by-Step Guidance: Detailed implementation instructions on SAP Discovery Center  

Knowledge Building Tools: Collaborate and learn how to use Business AI to implement use cases with certification programs and hands-on developer tutorials 

  • Tutorials: Prompt LLMs in the generative AI hub in SAP AI Core & Launchpad | SAP Tutorials  
  • Certification Programs: Recognized learning paths for Business AI , SAP Certified Associate - SAP Generative AI Developer 
  • Developer Videos: Hands-on videos on BTP use cases with AI  
  • Community: Be part of our new public group for AI enthusiasts  

Expert Assistance: Find experts to support your BTP AI journey 

  • SAP Certified Partners: Find SAP Partners  
  • Tailored SAP Support: SAP Support Portal  

While Sapphire Orlando has over 700 sessions and small events to choose from, we are also excited to share some sessions at Sapphire that showcase the value of AI for developers and practitioners from the SAP 2025 session catalog. To get started we recommend the following sessions that showcase new AI capabilities in SAP Build, ABAP and Integration suite in SAP BTP.  

AI sessions in BTP: 

Session 

Date and Time 

Location 

AI in SAP BTP: What’s new and what’s next - BTP1584  

 

Option1: May 20th, 11:00 am to 11:20 am  

Option 2: May 21st, 2:00 pm to 2:30 pm 

Option 1: Theater 22 

Option 2: Theater 24 

Joule Studio in action: Create AI agents and skills for business workflows - BTP2889 

May 20th, 11:30 am to 11:50 am 

Theater 26 

Building smarter AI agents with Joule Studio - BTP2677 

May 21st, 11:00 am to 11:20 am 

Theater 22

May 20th, 4:00 pm to 4:20 pm

Accelerate development and automation with SAP Build - BTP2888 

May 21st, 11:00 am to 11:20 am 

Theater 24 

AI agents with agent builder and runtime on SAP BTP - BTP2574 

May 20th, 3:00 pm to 3:40 pm  

(ASUG) S330D 

Future-proof your integration investment with AI - BTP2121 

May 20th, 4:00 pm to 4:20 pm 

Theater 24 

Now live: Build faster with Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities - BTP2179 

May 20th, 3pm to 3:20 pm 

Theater 23 

SAP Build: AI-driven innovation and extensibility for SAP Business Suite - BTP2592 

May 20th, 1:00 pm-1:20 pm 

Theater 24 

Your business, your way: SAP Build extensions, automations, and AI agents - BTP2667 

May 20th, 3:00 pm to 3:40 pm 

(ASUG) S330ABC 

 

SAP’s AI offerings benefit the enterprise. There are plenty of sessions to choose from the Business AI track. Here are some sessions that highlight the value and use cases of custom AI solutions in enterprise management:  

 

Session 

Date and time 

Location 

AI today: Reimagining efficiency with SAP Business AI and Joule Agents - BAI2616 

May 20th, 11:00 am to 11:20 am 

Theater 15 

AI tomorrow: Uncover what’s next with SAP Business AI - BAI2618 

May 21st, 2:30 pm to 2:50 pm 

Theater 15 

Exploring SAP Business AI and its road map - BAI3499 

May 20th , 1:30 am to 11:10 am 

(ASUG) S329 

Best practices for adopting Joule agents: Insights from experts - BAI2601

Generative AI hub: Instant access to top AI - BAI3411 

May 21st, 1:30 pm to 1:50 pm 

Theater 21 

Joule agents in action: Business like never before - BAI2606

Theater 21 

Joule: Unlock the future of productivity - BAI2597 

May 20th, 2:00 pm to 2:20 pm 

Theater 30 

Bringing AI to tabular data with innovative and harmonized services - BAI2626 

May 20th, 4:30 pm to 4:50 pm 

Theater 5 

Responsible AI in action: Building trust and driving innovation - BAI2612 

May 20th, 3:30 pm to 3:50 pm 

Theater 21 

Enhancing SAP software projects with AI-driven guidance and code generation - BAI3412 

May 20th, 4:30 pm to 4:50 pm 

Theater 23 

Discover recent and upcoming AI innovations for your business functions - BAI3956
